\begin{document}

\title{Domed Monuments Cluster at 3\textdegree{} Longitude Harmonics:\\
A Longitude Enrichment Test on the UNESCO Cultural and Mixed Heritage Corpus}

\author{Seth Tenenbaum\thanks{ORCID: \href{https://orcid.org/0009-0008-5797-2498}{0009-0008-5797-2498}.
Correspondence: seth@fourthtemple.com}\\
Independent Scholar, USA}

\maketitle

\abstract{
Among UNESCO domed monuments ($N=\NcircTotal$), \NcircTierApp{} sites
(\NcircAppRate\%) fall within $\AppThreshDeg{}\textdegree{}$
(${\lesssim}\AppThreshKm{}$~km) of a 3\textdegree{} longitude harmonic
referenced to Mount Gerizim—$\circEnrichApp\times$ the uniform null—surviving
full Bonferroni correction at $k=\NtotalTests$
($p=\pCircAppFisher$~\pCircAppFisherSig{}).
The full corpus ($N=\NclusterTotal$) independently confirms the period
(Bonferroni $p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestOne$, $k=\BonfK$); cluster asymmetry
is also significant ($p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestThree$).
The 3\textdegree{} period is recovered without specifying an anchor in
advance (Rayleigh permutation $p=\rayleighPermP$). The pipeline is fully
reproducible.
}

\bigskip

\noindent\textbf{Keywords:}
longitude periodicity, spatial statistics, Levant, UNESCO World Heritage,
3-degree harmonics, domed architecture, archaeoastronomy,
monument distribution, von Mises mixture, Mount Gerizim

\bigskip
